subject: DevOps
title: DaemonSets
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: DaemonSets.

hook.text: You need a log collector on every node. Not some nodes. Every single node. That's what a DaemonSet does.

buildup.text: A DaemonSet ensures one pod runs on each node. Add a new node, and the DaemonSet places a pod there automatically.

discovery.text: Log agents, monitoring exporters, and network plugins are classic DaemonSet workloads. One per node, always.

twist.text: You can scope DaemonSets to specific nodes using labels. Only GPU nodes get the GPU monitoring daemon, for example.

climax.text: DaemonSets update pods one node at a time by default. Rolling updates keep the cluster stable during upgrades.

punchline.text: DaemonSets: one pod per node, everywhere, always running.

quiz.question: What does a DaemonSet guarantee?
options: Exactly one pod runs on each selected node in the cluster
All pods run on a single node for performance
Pods are created only when traffic increases
